skill.name: background-removal
skill.description: Remove image backgrounds using the verging.ai API and return transparent PNG results from local files or image URLs.
skill.summary: Remove image backgrounds and create transparent PNG files through the verging.ai API.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, credential, and filesystem indicators, but most are expected documentation for a curl-based API workflow. The confirmed risks are third-party image upload, API key exposure in shell commands, remote URL downloading, and temporary file handling. No prompt injection, obfuscation, malicious exfiltration, or weak cryptography use was found in README.md or SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Upload Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to upload the selected image file to verging.ai through a multipart API request. This is legitimate for background removal, but it can disclose sensitive image content to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The create-job curl command posts an image file to the verging.ai API. The behavior is intentional, but the privacy impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: API Key Used in Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents VERGING_API_KEY and uses it inside curl Authorization headers. Environment-variable use is normal, but expanded command arguments can expose secrets through shell history, logs, or process inspection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key is explicitly required and passed to curl in request headers. This is a common pattern, but it creates practical credential-handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Remote Image Download to Temporary Directory
medium_findings.description: The workflow allows remote image URLs and downloads them with curl into /tmp/verging-bg-removal. This can retrieve untrusted content and should be restricted to expected image files and safe output paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented flow directly downloads a caller-provided URL to a fixed temporary directory. The skill also documents validation, which lowers but does not remove the risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command detections are in markdown code fences and inline examples. They are not Ruby backtick execution inside a runnable script, but they still document commands the agent may run.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown usage examples, not executable Ruby code. The files scanned are README.md and SKILL.md, with no script file present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the vendor website and API endpoints needed for authentication, job creation, and polling. No unrelated endpoint or covert destination was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All observed URLs are verging.ai URLs or user-supplied example image URLs. This matches the declared API integration purpose.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptography on lines that contain descriptive text or markdown table content. No hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic operations. The finding appears to be a pattern-matching false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing Network Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented curl requests combine outbound network access with Authorization headers. This pattern is necessary for the API but must be limited to the intended verging.ai endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples clearly send authenticated requests over the network. The target is expected, so this is a controlled dangerous pattern rather than confirmed abuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Remote Download
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow downloads a provided URL with curl. Agents should verify the URL, content type, size, and destination before downloading or uploading the file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern accepts a URL supplied by the user. The skill includes validation guidance, but the fetch target can still be untrusted.

content.value_statement: Manual background removal is slow when teams need clean product or profile images. This skill sends an image to verging.ai and returns a transparent PNG result.
actual_capabilities: Removes backgrounds from local JPG, PNG, and WebP images.
Accepts remote image URLs and downloads them before processing.
Creates background-removal jobs through the verging.ai API.
Polls job status until the result is completed or failed.
Returns a result URL and can download the transparent PNG output.
limitations: Requires a valid VERGING_API_KEY and available verging.ai credits.
Uploads image content to a third-party service for processing.
Supports images up to 10 MB in JPG, PNG, or WebP format.
Result quality depends on the source image and the external API.

use_cases.title: Prepare Product Photos
use_cases.description: Remove backgrounds from catalog images before placing products on clean layouts or marketplace listings.

use_cases.title: Create Profile Assets
use_cases.description: Turn portraits into transparent PNG files for bios, slides, cards, and team pages.

use_cases.title: Automate Design Handoffs
use_cases.description: Generate cutout images from approved source files during asset preparation workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Remove One Local Background
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single local file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from ./photo.jpg and show me the transparent PNG result URL.

prompt_templates.title: Process an Image URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a remote image and download the result.
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from this image URL and download the transparent PNG to the current folder: https://example.com/photo.png

prompt_templates.title: Save to a Named Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a predictable output path for later use.
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from ./assets/headshot.webp and save the result as ./assets/headshot-transparent.png.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Before Processing
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with safety checks before upload.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check that ./campaign/product.png is under 10 MB, confirm it is a supported image type, then remove the background and save the PNG result.

output_examples.input: A local product photo named shoe.jpg needs a transparent background.
output_examples.output: The skill validates the file, submits it to verging.ai, waits for completion, and returns a transparent PNG download link.

output_examples.input: A team portrait from a public image URL should be saved locally after background removal.
output_examples.output: The skill downloads the source image, creates a background-removal job, and saves the completed transparent PNG to the requested path.

output_examples.input: A WebP marketing asset must be checked before processing.
output_examples.output: The skill confirms format and size limits before upload, then reports the job result or failure status.

best_practices: Use environment variables for API keys and avoid pasting secrets into shared prompts.
Process only images you are allowed to upload to verging.ai.
Review remote URLs and file sizes before downloading or submitting images.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential, regulated, or customer-sensitive images without approval.
Do not place real API keys directly in prompts, tickets, or public repositories.
Do not bypass file type and size validation for remote downloads.

faq.question: Does this skill work without an API key?
faq.answer: No. It requires VERGING_API_KEY and available verging.ai credits.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The documented formats are JPG, JPEG, PNG, and WebP, with a 10 MB maximum file size.

faq.question: Does it upload my image to another service?
faq.answer: Yes. The image is uploaded to verging.ai so the external API can remove the background.

faq.question: Can it process image URLs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can download a remote image URL before submitting the file for processing.

faq.question: What output does it create?
faq.answer: It returns a transparent PNG result URL and can download the PNG to a requested local path.

faq.question: Is this safe for private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Private images should only be processed when your policy allows upload to verging.ai.
